symbols.name: \(m\)
symbols.def: common support cardinality of each candidate treatment proxy

symbols.name: \(u\)
symbols.def: support cardinality of the latent confounder

symbols.name: \(\gamma\)
symbols.def: cardinality of the selected valid treatment-proxy subset

symbols.name: \(\mathcal Z_i\)
symbols.def: \(\mathcal Z_i=\{1,\ldots,m\}\), the support of proxy coordinate \(i\)

symbols.name: \(Y(0),Y(1)\)
symbols.def: potential outcomes under treatment levels zero and one

symbols.name: \(P\)
symbols.def: law of \(Y(0),Y(1),W,U,A,Z\), with expectations and marginals denoted by \(P\)

symbols.name: \(\nu\)
symbols.def: index set in a fortified conditional-mean restriction

symbols.name: \(\mathcal A_\gamma\)
symbols.def: family of subsets of \([K]\) having cardinality \(\gamma\)

symbols.name: \(d\)
symbols.def: generic function of treatment and candidate proxies

symbols.name: \(E_A^0\)
symbols.def: kernel of the treatment-coordinate sum map

symbols.name: \(E_i^0\)
symbols.def: kernel of the coordinate-\(i\) sum map on \(\mathbb R^m\)

symbols.name: \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}\)
symbols.def: pairing map from demand functions to linear functionals on the supply space

symbols.name: \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)\)
symbols.def: matrix of \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}\) with demand basis elements as rows and supply basis elements as columns

symbols.name: \(h_{K,m}(\gamma)\)
symbols.def: dimension of the fortified conditional-mean-zero space

symbols.name: \(D_{K,m}(\gamma)\)
symbols.def: dimension of the selected-component fortified supply space

symbols.name: \(Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\)
symbols.def: dimension of the selected-component fortified demand space

symbols.name: \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\)
symbols.def: first selected-subset cardinality with nonnegative slack

novel.justification: This full-cell support condition holds in finite latent measurement models generated by strictly positive tables for \(U,A,Z\). It is a linear-algebra regularity condition: it fixes ambient cell dimensions and makes multiplication by \(p_{AZ}\) invertible.

standard.name: latent ignorability conditional on selected valid proxies' complement
treatment-proxy exclusion for a selected valid subset
outcome-proxy exclusion for a selected valid subset

definitions.name: \(E_A^0,E_i^0\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathbb R^{\{0,1\}}=\langle\mathbf1_A\rangle\oplus E_A^0\), where \(E_A^0=\ker(x\mapsto\sum_{a=0}^1x_a)\), and \(\mathbb R^m=\langle\mathbf1_i\rangle\oplus E_i^0\), where \(E_i^0=\ker(x\mapsto\sum_{z_i=1}^m x_{z_i})\). Thus \(\dim E_A^0=1\) and \(\dim E_i^0=m-1\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal H_\gamma(P)\)
definitions.construction:
\(\mathcal H_\gamma(P)=\{d\in L_P^2(A,Z):\mathbb E_P[d(A,Z)\mid Z_{-
u}]=0	ext{ for every }
u\subseteq[K]	ext{ with }|
u|=\gamma\}\)

definitions.name: \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}:\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\to\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)^\ast\), with \([\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}g](s)=\mathbb E_P[s(A,Z)g(U,A,C_\alpha)]\).

definitions.name: \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)\)
definitions.construction: \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=[\mathbb E_P\{s_j(A,Z)g_i(U,A,C_\alpha)\}]_{i,j}\), where \((g_i)_i\) is a basis of \(\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\) and \((s_j)_j\) is a basis of \(\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)\).

definitions.name: compatible positive-minor handle
definitions.construction: For fixed \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), parameterize strictly positive finite conditional tables for \(U,A,Z\), couple \(Y(0),Y(1),W\) conditionally on \(U,C_\alpha\), impose the member properties defining \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\), and seek a \(Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\)-minor of \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)\) whose numerator polynomial is not identically zero on that compatible completion parameter space. When \(\gamma=K\), also record the two \(m^K\)-by-\(u\) conditional-probability matrices \(\Pi_a=[P(U=v\mid A=a,Z=z)]_{z,v}\), one for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), as the endpoint block variables. For \(\gamma<K\), retain the maximal-minor search on the full fortified supply matrix.

statements.statement:
For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), and full-data law \(P\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\), the map \(d\mapsto q_d\) sends \(\mathcal H_\gamma(P)\) bijectively onto \[\Bigl(E_A^0\otimes igotimes_{i=1}^K\mathbb R^m\Bigr)\oplus\Biggl[\langle\mathbf1_Aangle\otimes igoplus_{\substack{S\subseteq[K]\|S|>K-\gamma}}\left( igotimes_{i\in S}E_i^0ight)\otimes\left( igotimes_{i
otin S}\langle\mathbf1_iangleight)\Biggr].\] Equivalently, \(\mathbb E_P[d\mid Z_{-
u}]=0\) holds exactly when \(\sum_{a,z_
u}q_d(a,z)=0\) for every \(
u\subseteq[K]\) with \(|
u|=\gamma\). Hence \(\dim\mathcal H_\gamma(P)=h_{K,m}(\gamma)\). Moreover, \(\mathcal H_\gamma(P)\cap L_P^2(C_\alpha)=\{0\}\), so \(\dim\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)=D_{K,m}(\gamma)\) and \(\dim\mathcal G_\alpha(P)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\).
statements.justification: The weighted-array decomposition gives the exact fortified supply dimension and the fixed tensor coordinates used in both directions of the rank characterization.
statements.gap: The anchor states simultaneous conditional-mean restrictions but does not derive their finite-support tensor decomposition under dependent proxy coordinates.
statements.consumer: The resulting dimensions compute \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\), and the orthogonal complement is the starting point for the compatible positive-minor construction.

statements.statement: For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), and full-data law \(P\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\), if \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)<0\), then \(\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)<Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\). Thus \(\ker\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}\ne\{0\}\), and the Assumption-5 fortified pairing is noninjective for every such \(P,\alpha\). In particular, no law \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) that also satisfies Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) can satisfy that outcome-completeness premise.
statements.justification: Negative supply-minus-demand slack is the necessity half of the exact characterization and rules out injectivity uniformly over full-cell laws.
statements.gap: The anchor assumes fortified completeness but does not identify cardinality regimes where its pairing premise fails at every law in a selected full-cell component.
statements.consumer: Together with Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:compatible-minor-attainment}\), it makes the sign of \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) an exact feasibility decision.

statements.statement: For fixed \(K,m,u\), \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma+1)>\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) for every \(\gamma\in\{1,\ldots,K-1\}\). Hence \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\), when finite, is the unique nonnegative-slack boundary, and every selected valid-subset component whose cardinality is strictly below \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\) has the universal rank barrier in Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:universal-rank-barrier}\) at each of its laws satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\).
statements.justification: Strict monotonicity converts the pointwise sign characterization into a single valid-proxy-count threshold.
statements.gap: The anchor varies \(\gamma\) without deriving an ordered arithmetic boundary for the fortified pairing premise.
statements.consumer: Together with Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:compatible-minor-attainment}\), the proposition shows that \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\), when finite, is precisely the first cardinality with compatible generic row-rank attainment.

statements.statement: For \(K=2\), \(m=3\), \(u=2\), and \(\gamma=1\), \(h_{2,3}(1)=13\), \(D_{2,3}(1)=16\), and \(Q_{2,3,2}(1)=12\). Therefore \(M_1(P,\alpha)\) has \(12\) demand rows and \(16\) supply columns, and outcome completeness requires row rank \(12\).
statements.justification: The calculation audits the motivating nonbinary example and includes the complete complement-proxy supply dimension.
statements.gap: The \(12\)-by-\(15\) illustration in Yu, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen \cite{YuShiTchetgen2025} omits one direction from the ternary complement space; the corrected pairing is \(12\)-by-\(16\).
statements.consumer: Because its slack is positive, Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:compatible-minor-attainment}\) certifies compatible generic row rank \(12\), not merely dimensional possibility.

statements.statement: For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), and \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\) with \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), does some \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) attain \(\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\)? If so, is row-rank attainment generic outside a proper algebraic subset of the compatible completion parameter space in \(\mathrm{def:compatible-minor-handle}\)?
statements.justification: Dimension compatibility is necessary but may not be sufficient because selected-validity restrictions can force every maximal minor to vanish.
statements.gap: AllmanMatiasRhodes2009 and Gu2025BlessingDependence show why a positive-minor argument, not row counting, is required in constrained discrete latent models. YuShiTchetgen2025's Theorem 2.1 uses Assumptions 3, 4, and 5 together with Assumption 1 and consistency, but does not ask whether its Assumption-5 fortified pairing generically attains row rank under compatible selected-subset restrictions.
statements.consumer: A positive answer turns the necessary arithmetic frontier into an exact, decidable generic feasibility threshold for the fortified outcome-bridge route.

sampling_model.design: An independent sample \(O_i=(Y_i,A_i,W_i,Z_i)\sim P_{Y,A,W,Z}\), \(i=1,\ldots,n\), is observed.
sampling_model.units: Each unit has latent state \(U_i\) and potential outcomes \(Y_i(0),Y_i(1)\).
sampling_model.overlap: Full-cell support requires \(P(U,A,Z)>0\) at every finite cell. It therefore implies \(0<P(A=a\mid U,C_\alpha)<1\) almost surely for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\); no separate treatment-positivity assumption is imposed.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: This is an identification and impossibility proposal; it makes no sampling-error or estimator-rate claim.

bibliography.citation: M. Yu, X. Shi, and E. J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2025), "Fortified Proximal Causal Inference with Many Invalid Proxies," arXiv:2506.13152.
W. Miao, Z. Geng, and E. J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018), "Identifying Causal Effects With Proxy Variables of an Unmeasured Confounder," Biometrika 105, 987--993.
J. Zhang, W. Li, W. Miao, and E. J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023), "Proximal Causal Inference without Uniqueness Assumptions," Statistics and Probability Letters 198, 109836.
E. S. Allman, C. Matias, and J. A. Rhodes (2009), "Identifiability of Parameters in Latent Structure Models with Many Observed Variables," Annals of Statistics 37, 3099--3132.
Y. Gu (2025), "Blessing of Dependence: Identifiability and Geometry of Discrete Latent Variable Models," Bernoulli 31(2), 948--972.

G. Duarte, N. Finkelstein, D. Knox, J. Mummolo, and I. Shpitser (2024), "An Automated Approach to Causal Inference in Discrete Settings," Journal of the American Statistical Association 119, 1778--1793.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Yu et al. use Assumption 3, Assumption 4, Assumption 5, and Assumption 1 with consistency to obtain ATE identification in Theorem 2.1.
comparator_promise_table.matched_by: Theorem thm:universal-rank-barrier: on a selected cardinality-\(\gamma\) full-cell component with negative slack, Assumption 5 cannot hold at any such law; this neither refutes Assumption 4 nor establishes ATE nonidentification or excludes alternative bridge routes.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen give finite-proxy rank conditions for proximal identification with known-valid proxies.
Zhang et al. show that proximal causal functionals can remain identified without uniqueness of bridge solutions.
Allman, Matias, and Rhodes use nonvanishing minors to establish generic identifiability in finite latent-variable models.
Gu characterizes generic identifiability and geometry in discrete latent-variable models under structural dependence.
Duarte et al. give sharp polynomial optimization for specified discrete structural causal models.

tldr: The supply-minus-demand slack \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) is an exact arithmetic feasibility diagnostic for the fortified outcome-completeness pairing on every selected cardinality-\(\gamma\) component with full-cell support. For every \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), a compatible law with injective pairing exists if and only if \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\); throughout that feasible region, full row rank holds off a proper algebraic exceptional set. Negative slack forces noninjectivity at every full-cell law. The slack is strictly increasing, its endpoint is \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(K)=2(m^K-u)\), and therefore \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)<+\infty\) exactly when \(u\le m^K\); at \(u=m^K\), the threshold is \(K\). These are feasibility results for the Assumption-5 outcome-bridge route, not an identifying functional for \(\tau\), a proof of bridge existence, or an average-treatment-effect nonidentification theorem.

project_justification.gap: Yu, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen \cite{YuShiTchetgen2025} use the fortified completeness premise in their outcome-bridge identification route but treat the defended valid-proxy count \(\gamma\) as a sensitivity index without deriving when the corresponding finite pairing can be injective. A supply-versus-demand count gives necessity, but constrained discrete latent models require a compatible nonvanishing-minor construction for sufficiency and an endpoint calculation to determine whether the arithmetic frontier exists at all.
project_justification.niche: The selected finite-support model admits a law-independent weighted-array decomposition of the fortified supply. Its low-order orthogonal complement has enough block symmetry to construct positive conditional-probability tables for every nonnegative-slack case, while the endpoint identity \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(K)=2(m^K-u)\) gives a complete existence test for the threshold.
project_justification.fill: The paper proves that compatible full-cell injectivity exists exactly when \(D_{K,m}(\gamma)\ge Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\), equivalently \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), for every \(\gamma\) and every selected \(\alpha\). It also proves generic attainment in the feasible region, universal failure in the negative-slack region, strict monotonicity, and the endpoint characterization \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)<+\infty\) if and only if \(u\le m^K\), with \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,m^K)=K\).

related_work: Yu, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen \cite{YuShiTchetgen2025} combine their Assumptions 3, 4, and 5 with Assumption 1 and consistency in an outcome-bridge formula for the average treatment effect. The present result isolates the finite-support feasibility of their Assumption-5 fortified pairing on a selected cardinality-\(\gamma\) component with full-cell support and supplies an exact arithmetic threshold, including the endpoint criterion \(u\le m^K\) for finiteness, that their sensitivity sweep does not provide. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen \cite{MiaoGengTchetgen2018} give the categorical rank foundation for known valid proxies, while Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen \cite{ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023} distinguish bridge nonuniqueness from causal-functional nonidentification. Allman, Matias, and Rhodes \cite{AllmanMatiasRhodes2009} motivate the nonvanishing-minor route to generic identification, and Gu \cite{Gu2025BlessingDependence} shows why latent-model restrictions can invalidate naive dimension counting. Here a compatible positive-minor construction proves that the count is sufficient for every \(\gamma\). Duarte, Finkelstein, Knox, Mummolo, and Shpitser \cite{DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024} study sharp polynomial optimization in specified discrete structural causal models; this paper instead gives an analytic rank-feasibility theorem and makes no polynomial-program or identified-set claim for the average treatment effect.
interpretation: The displayed quantity \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) is a feasibility diagnostic, not an identifying functional or recovery map for \(\tau\). An analyst who defends \(\gamma\) valid treatment proxies can compute its sign directly. Negative slack is a law-uniform certificate that no full-cell law in the selected component can satisfy the fortified outcome-completeness pairing. Nonnegative slack means that the premise is compatible and holds generically in the positive factor-table parameterization, although exceptional algebraic laws can still fail it. Strict monotonicity makes \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\) the exact transition when it is finite. The endpoint formula shows that no transition exists when \(u>m^K\), whereas \(u=m^K\) forces the transition to occur only at \(\gamma=K\).
technical_internal_limitation: The equivalence is stated for a selected cardinality-\(\gamma\) component and requires full support on every \((U,A,Z)\) cell. Genericity is relative to the positive compatible factor-table coordinates; the proper algebraic exceptional set can contain substantively important laws, so nonnegative slack does not assert injectivity at every law. The endpoint result only decides whether the arithmetic feasibility frontier is finite and where it lies when \(u=m^K\). The results concern only the Assumption-5 pairing in the outcome-bridge route of Yu, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen \cite{YuShiTchetgen2025}; they do not establish the remaining premises of that route, prove existence of an outcome bridge, identify \(\tau\), prove average-treatment-effect nonidentification below the frontier, or exclude other proximal identification strategies.
honest_scope: For fixed \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), the coordinates in \(\alpha\) satisfy the selected proxy restrictions relative to \(C_\alpha\); coordinates outside \(\alpha\) are not thereby declared invalid. Within that selected component and under full-cell support, the pairing-rank question is settled for every \(\gamma\): existence and generic attainment hold if and only if \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), and negative slack gives universal noninjectivity. The threshold is finite exactly for \(u\le m^K\), and equals \(K\) at the boundary \(u=m^K\). The scope remains route-level and algebraic: no claim is made that another completeness premise fails, that the average treatment effect is unidentified, that alternative proximal routes fail, or that a sharp identified set is represented by a finite polynomial program.
